message: beads: close bd-48wuw embedded NUL validation

files.patch:
@@ -443,7 +443,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-3yy9k","title":"[HIGH] ffs-fuse lacks a short-v1-buffer regression for fscrypt policy-ex v2 replies","description":"Recent fscrypt `_EX` review found that `crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs:1652-1679` only rejects buffers smaller than `header + v1`, but can still emit a full v2 payload when the caller advertises exactly `header + v1` bytes. The current unit coverage in `crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs:5165-5208` only exercises the "smaller than v1" rejection path and the full-size v2 happy path, so the actual overflow contract break can regress unnoticed.

Acceptance:
- Add a unit or mounted-path regression that advertises `FSCRYPT_POLICY_EX_HEADER_SIZE + FSCRYPT_POLICY_V1_SIZE` while the backend returns a v2 policy.
- The test must fail on the current buggy behavior and pass only once the ioctl clamps or rejects the oversized reply correctly.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:31:42.013106273Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T03:21:42.016891337Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T03:21:42.014542520Z","close_reason":"Regression test dispatch_ioctl_get_encryption_policy_ex_v2_rejects_v1_sized_buffer (crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs:5434-5472) advertises FSCRYPT_POLICY_EX_HEADER_SIZE+FSCRYPT_POLICY_V1_SIZE while the backend returns a v2 policy, asserts ERANGE, and verifies backend was consulted before rejection. Passes green via rch workspace run.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ffs-fuse","fscrypt","regression","review","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-43w8","title":"Harden ext4 write-path edge-case tests in ffs-core","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:22:08.567984237Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-26T07:17:17.605907065Z","closed_at":"2026-02-26T07:17:17.605888129Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-46fmd","title":"add mounted-path ext4 create-and-mkdir under non-directory parent ENOTDIR coverage","description":"Survey finding: ext4 mounted-path FUSE E2E proves create and mkdir success, but the operator-visible refusal contract for attempting either operation under a regular-file parent is only frozen in ffs-core unit tests. Scope: add ext4 mounted-path probes that attempt file creation and mkdir beneath a non-directory parent and assert exact ENOTDIR with unchanged parent contents. Acceptance: fuse_e2e freezes ext4 create/mkdir non-directory-parent refusal behavior.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:14:12.818165727Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T22:44:29.748728166Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T22:44:29.746438255Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-48wuw","title":"Reject embedded NUL path components in filesystem writes","description":"Reviewer finding [HIGH]: ffs-core accepts embedded NUL bytes in user-supplied path components for mutating filesystem operations, and the latest path-component fuzz seed commit explicitly pins that acceptance as desired behavior.

Evidence:
- crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:14500 validate_single_path_component only rejects empty components, embedded slash, and names longer than 255 bytes. It returns Ok for names containing b"\0".
- The validator feeds ext4 and btrfs write paths before directory mutation: ext4 create/mknod/mkdir/unlink/link/symlink/rename, and btrfs create/mkdir/unlink/rename/link/symlink.
- fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_path_component_validation.rs:129-148 models only Empty, ContainsSlash, TooLong, and Valid. Embedded NUL bytes therefore classify as Valid.
- Commit e0d2a78 adds fuzz/corpus/fuzz_path_component_validation/seed_embedded_nul containing NUL bytes and the commit message says validator currently accepts them and pins that behavior.

Why this matters:
POSIX path components cannot contain NUL because path strings are NUL-terminated at the syscall boundary. Letting internal write APIs create NUL-containing names can produce entries that normal userspace paths cannot address reliably, and can diverge from kernel ext4/btrfs behavior even though the mounted FUSE surface is supposed to preserve Linux pathname semantics.

Expected behavior:
Reject any path component containing b"\0" before inode allocation or directory mutation, across ext4 and btrfs create, mkdir, mknod, link, symlink, unlink/rmdir as applicable, and rename old/new components. Update the path-component fuzz oracle so NUL-containing names are invalid rather than valid.

Reproduction direction:
Add regression tests that attempt create/mkdir/link/symlink/rename with OsStr::from_bytes values containing embedded NUL under both ext4 and btrfs test filesystems. Assert each operation fails and the source/directory state is unchanged. Add a fuzz corpus seed that expects rejection for embedded-NUL names.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-29T12:28:24.674392507Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-29T20:00:03.568074008Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ffs-core","path-validation","review"]}

- The validator feeds ext4 and btrfs write paths before directory mutation: ext4 create/mknod/mkdir/unlink/link/symlink/rename, and btrfs create/mkdir/unlink/rename/link/symlink.
- fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_path_component_validation.rs:129-148 models only Empty, ContainsSlash, TooLong, and Valid. Embedded NUL bytes therefore classify as Valid.
- Commit e0d2a78 adds fuzz/corpus/fuzz_path_component_validation/seed_embedded_nul containing NUL bytes and the commit message says validator currently accepts them and pins that behavior.

Why this matters:
POSIX path components cannot contain NUL because path strings are NUL-terminated at the syscall boundary. Letting internal write APIs create NUL-containing names can produce entries that normal userspace paths cannot address reliably, and can diverge from kernel ext4/btrfs behavior even though the mounted FUSE surface is supposed to preserve Linux pathname semantics.

Expected behavior:
Reject any path component containing b"\0" before inode allocation or directory mutation, across ext4 and btrfs create, mkdir, mknod, link, symlink, unlink/rmdir as applicable, and rename old/new components. Update the path-component fuzz oracle so NUL-containing names are invalid rather than valid.

Reproduction direction:
Add regression tests that attempt create/mkdir/link/symlink/rename with OsStr::from_bytes values containing embedded NUL under both ext4 and btrfs test filesystems. Assert each operation fails and the source/directory state is unchanged. Add a fuzz corpus seed that expects rejection for embedded-NUL names.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-29T12:28:24.674392507Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-29T20:08:39.819584886Z","closed_at":"2026-04-29T20:08:39.817092387Z","close_reason":"Completed: central validator already rejects embedded NUL; added ext4/btrfs regression coverage for mknod/unlink/rmdir/link/symlink and rename old/new names; fuzz oracle check passes","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ffs-core","path-validation","review"]}
 {"id":"bd-4b81b","title":"xfstests regression gate should not exempt likely-pass allowlist entries","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T18:11:16.084614543Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T18:17:17.624000042Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T18:17:17.621536407Z","close_reason":"Fixed regression gate allowlist status semantics; likely-pass no longer masks pass-to-fail drift.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
